headline: Is an Oil Shock Enough to Send the U.S. Into Recession? Exxon Mobil’s Chief Economist Answers.
summary: Stories about recessions frequently adhere to a boom-to-bust narrative arc.  Real life is less faithful to that neat story line, according to Tyler Goodspeed, a Harvard and Cambridge-trained economic historian and the chief economist of  Exxon Mobil  “Recessions are unpredictable,” he says.  There is nothing innate to human psychology or inherent in modern economies that indicates recessions are part of an inevitable business cycle, he writes in his new book, Recession.
